Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class ResponseBody

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.ResponseBody
public final class ResponseBody extends Object
A fluent accessor for an HTTP response body returned by RestResponse.

Provides convenient methods for reading the body as a string, byte array, or stream. The body is read lazily; each method reads and returns the body content once.

Obtain instances via RestResponse.body().

Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

See Also:
  • juneau-ng REST client
Since:
9.2.1

  • Method Details

    • asString

      public String asString() throws IOException
      Returns the response body as a UTF-8 string.
      Returns:
      The body string, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body.

    • asString

      public String asString(Charset charset) throws IOException
      Returns the response body decoded with the given charset.
      Parameters:
      charset - The character set to use for decoding. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The body string, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body.

    • asBytes

      public byte[] asBytes() throws IOException
      Returns the response body as a byte array.
      Returns:
      The body bytes, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body.

    • asStream

      public InputStream asStream()
      Returns the raw response body stream.

      Callers should not close this stream directly; close the parent RestResponse instead.

      Returns:
      The body stream, or null if the response has no body.

    • readAllBytes

      public byte[] readAllBytes() throws IOException
      Reads the response body into a byte array and returns it, or returns null if there is no body.

      Equivalent to asBytes().

      Returns:
      The body bytes, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body.

    • asCursor

      public <T> T asCursor(Class<T> type) throws IOException
      Opens a token/record-streaming cursor over the response body using the parser negotiated from the response Content-Type.

      Equivalent to asCursor(negotiatedParser, type) where the parser is selected from the response Content-Type header; when no registered parser matches and no default parser is configured, a 415 Unsupported Media Type is thrown. Use asCursor(Parser, Class) to read with an explicit parser.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The cursor type (RecordReader, TokenReader, or a concrete subtype).
      Parameters:
      type - The declared cursor type. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A cursor over the live response body. Never null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the body is missing, the parser does not support the requested cursor surface, or the produced cursor is not assignable to type.

    • as

      public <T> T as(Class<T> type) throws IOException
      Parses the response body to type using the parser negotiated from the response Content-Type.

      When the header is absent or matches no registered parser and no default parser is configured, a 415 Unsupported Media Type is thrown. Use as(Parser, Class) to force a specific parser, bypassing content negotiation.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The type to parse to.
      Parameters:
      type - The type to parse to. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The parsed body, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body or the body could not be parsed.

    • as

      public <T> T as(Parser parser, Class<T> type) throws IOException
      Parses the response body to type using the given parser.

      This forces the supplied parser, bypassing the Content-Type negotiation performed by as(Class).

      A parse failure is surfaced strictly as an IOException wrapping the underlying ParseException; the malformed body is never returned to the caller.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The type to parse to.
      Parameters:
      parser - The parser to use. Must not be null.
      type - The type to parse to. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The parsed body, or null if the response has no body.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs reading the body or the body could not be parsed.

    • asCursor

      public <T> T asCursor(Parser parser, Class<T> type) throws IOException
      Opens a token/record-streaming cursor over the response body using the given parser.

      This forces the supplied parser, bypassing the Content-Type negotiation performed by asCursor(Class).

      The cursor reads directly from the live response stream — the body is not buffered into memory. The caller owns the returned cursor and the parent RestResponse; close them when done.

      When type is (or extends) TokenReader the parser must implement TokenReadable; otherwise it must implement RecordReadable.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The cursor type (RecordReader, TokenReader, or a concrete subtype).
      Parameters:
      parser - The parser that opens the cursor. Must not be null.
      type - The declared cursor type. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A cursor over the live response body. Never null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the body is missing, the parser does not support the requested cursor surface, or the produced cursor is not assignable to type.
